Fixes #11046

prs.title: fix: null out effect.b in destroy_effect to prevent memory leak
prs.body:
## Fix: #17881

### Root Cause

When a dynamic component switches (e.g. `<Component />`), branch effects are destroyed via `destroy_effect()`.

However, the `effect.b` (Boundary reference) field was not cleared alongside other references (`next`, `prev`, `ctx`, `deps`, `fn`, `nodes`, `ac`).

As a result, destroyed effects retained a reference to the `Boundary` instance, which holds references to component state and child effects. This prevented destroyed component subtrees from being garbage collected, causing memory usage to grow during repeated component switching.

### Solution

Clear the boundary reference during effect destruction.

```diff
effect.next =
      effect.prev =
      effect.teardown =
      effect.ctx =
      effect.deps =
      effect.fn =
      effect.nodes =
      effect.ac =
+     effect.b =
          null;
```

### Test Plan

* All existing tests pass:

  * runtime-runes: 2,459
  * runtime-legacy: 3,294
  * signals: 96
  * Total: 5,849 tests

* Added a dynamic component switching test that toggles components repeatedly and verifies correct DOM output.

### Validation

Reproduced the issue using the example from #17881.

After applying the fix:

* Memory usage stabilizes during repeated component switching
* Destroyed components are properly reclaimed by the garbage collector
* No behavioral regressions observed

### Impact

* Fixes memory leak in dynamic component switching
* Minimal, safe change
* No API or behavior changes

prs.title: docs: clarify instance auth override behavior
prs.body:
Fixes #221

## Description

This PR clarifies the documentation around the `auth` property behavior when using axios instances. The issue reported that when an axios instance is created with an `auth` property configured, attempting to override it with a custom `Authorization` header in a request config silently fails because the instance's `auth` property takes precedence and overwrites the custom header.

## Changes

- Added documentation in the `auth` property section explaining that instance `auth` config overrides custom `Authorization` headers
- Added a new section "Overriding instance `auth` configuration" with clear examples showing:
  - The incorrect approach (which silently fails)
  - The correct approach (using `auth: null` to disable instance auth)

## Testing

The documentation now clearly explains the expected behavior and provides working examples for users who need to override instance-level auth configuration with custom Authorization headers.

prs.title: feat: add support for raw XML responses
prs.body: Fixes #2522 **Description:** - Adds support for raw XML responses without JSON encoding, resolving issue #2522 - Introduces `response.XML` type that allows handlers to return raw XML content with proper `Content-Type: application/xml` header - Previously, XML responses were either JSON-encoded or had incorrect content type headers - This change enables proper XML responses for integrations that require raw XML (e.g., callback URLs expecting XML) **Breaking Changes (if applicable):** - None. This is a new feature addition that doesn't break existing functionality. **Additional Information:** - Added `response.XML` type in `pkg/gofr/http/response/xml.go` - Updated `Responder.Respond()` method to handle XML responses similar to how `File` and `Template` responses work - Added comprehensive tests covering default and custom XML content types - Updated documentation with XML response usage examples - Screenshot attached showing curl output with correct `Content-Type: application/xml` header and raw XML body **Checklist:** - [x] I have formatted my code using `goimport` and `golangci-lint`. - [x] All new code is covered by unit tests. - [x] This PR does not decrease the overall code coverage. - [x] I have reviewed the code comments and documentation for clarity.

prs.title: feat(frontend): add Signup page
prs.body:
### PR Title
feat(frontend): add Signup page with Tailwind v4 and integrate with backend

### Summary
Implements a responsive, aesthetic Signup page in the new React (Vite) frontend and wires it to the existing backend signup endpoint. Adds CORS and a simple health check to the backend for browser access and diagnostics.

### What’s in this PR
- React app scaffolded in `frontend/` (Vite)
- Tailwind CSS v4 configured via `@tailwindcss/postcss`
- Centered “glass” Signup card with indigo→purple theme
- Client-side validation (required fields + password match)
- API integration: POST `http://localhost:3000/api/users/signup`
- Stores JWT token in `localStorage` on success
- Loading/disabled states + success message/modal
- Faster UX: reduced request timeout and shorter success modal duration
- Backend: enable CORS, add lightweight request logging, add `GET /api/health`

### Files of note
- `frontend/src/pages/Signup.jsx` – Signup UI + fetch logic
- `frontend/src/App.jsx` – Displays Signup page
- `frontend/src/index.css` – Tailwind v4 import
- `frontend/postcss.config.js` – Tailwind v4 PostCSS plugin
- `backend/src/index.js` – CORS, request logger, health route

### How to test
1) Backend:
   - Ensure `.env` has `DATABASE_URL`, `JWT_SECRET_KEY`, `JWT_EXPIRES_IN`
   - From `backend/`: `npx prisma generate && npx prisma migrate deploy`
   - Run: `npm run dev`
   - Visit `http://localhost:3000/api/health` → expect `{ status: 'ok' }`

2) Frontend:
   - From `frontend/`: `npm install && npm run dev`
   - Open the dev URL in the browser
   - Submit the form with a new email
   - Expect success banner/modal and `localStorage.auth_token` on real 201 response

### Environment variables
- Backend:
  - `DATABASE_URL` (MySQL)
  - `JWT_SECRET_KEY`
  - `JWT_EXPIRES_IN` (e.g., `7d`)

### Endpoints
- POST `/api/users/signup` – create user, returns `{ user, token }`
- GET `/api/health` – returns `{ status: 'ok' }` for sanity checks

### Notes
- For local demos when backend is slow/unavailable, the UI can show an optimistic success; remove or guard via env flag for production if desired.
- CORS is enabled broadly; tighten origins for production.

### Risks / Follow-ups
- Lock down CORS origin in production
- Add server-side validation errors surfaced to UI
- Redirect to dashboard/profile post-signup

### Checklist
- [x] Builds locally (frontend + backend)
- [x] Tailwind v4 configured with PostCSS
- [x] API integrated and tested
- [x] Token persisted on success
- [x] Lint passes

prs.title: Add Mini Budget Calculator for issue #848
prs.body:
Closes #848 - Create a Mini Budget Calculator

## Description
Implemented a Mini Budget Calculator that helps track weekly or monthly expenses by category.

### Features:
- Track expenses across multiple categories (Food, Transportation, Housing, etc.)
- Switch between weekly and monthly budget views
- View expense summaries with totals per category
- Simple command-line interface
- Persistent data storage (saves to JSON file)

### How to Test:
```bash
# Add an expense
python budget_calculator.py add 1500 Food --description "Weekly groceries"

# View summary
python budget_calculator.py summary

# Set budget period
python budget_calculator.py set-period weekly

prs.title: feat: Add Quick Sort visualization
prs.body:
## Quick Sort Visualization Implementation

### Description
This PR introduces an interactive Quick Sort visualization using Matplotlib, providing a clear visual representation of the Quick Sort algorithm in action.

### Features
- **Visual Elements**:
  - Yellow bar: Current pivot element
  - Red bars: Left and right pointers during partitioning
  - Blue bars: Active sub-array being processed
  - Grey bars: Inactive parts of the array

- **Interactive Visualization**:
  - Step-by-step animation of the sorting process
  - Real-time updates of array state
  - Visual feedback for each operation (swaps, comparisons, etc.)

### Technical Details
- Implemented as a Python generator function
- Uses Matplotlib's `FuncAnimation` for smooth visualization
- Includes detailed comments and documentation

### How to Test
1. Run the script:
   ```bash
   python quick_sort_visualization.py

#29

- [src/utils/soundEffects.js](cci:7://file:///Users/nitinsahu/Desktop/t-trce/T-race/src/utils/soundEffects.js:0:0-0:0) - Sound utility
- [src/pages/race.js](cci:7://file:///Users/nitinsahu/Desktop/t-trce/T-race/src/pages/race.js:0:0-0:0) - Race page integration

- [src/context/AuthContext.js](cci:7://file:///Users/nitinsahu/Desktop/t-trce/T-race/src/context/AuthContext.js:0:0-0:0) - Handle disabled Firebase gracefully

## Testing
1. Navigate to `/race` or `/practice` page
2. Click "Start Race!" or "Start Practice!" button
3. Experience 3-2-1-Go countdown with visual and audio effects
4. Verify timer starts only after "GO!" appears

Closes #87

prs.title: Fix content disposition header access
prs.body:
Fixes issue where Content-Disposition and other headers are visible in the browser Network tab but not accessible via response.headers in JavaScript code.

Problem:-
Due to CORS restrictions, XMLHttpRequest.getAllResponseHeaders() doesn't include certain headers like Content-Disposition, even though they're visible in the browser's Network tab and accessible via individual getResponseHeader() calls.

Solution:-
Enhanced the XHR adapter to:

First get headers via getAllResponseHeaders() (existing behavior)

Then try to get commonly needed headers individually via getResponseHeader()

Add any accessible headers that weren't included in the bulk response

Headers Now Accessible:

content-disposition

content-range

content-encoding

content-length

content-type

last-modified

date

etag

expires

cache-control

Testing:-
Verified fix works — Content-Disposition header is now accessible via response.headers['content-disposition'].

issues.title: Footer layout still looks visually inconsistent after recent sponsor layout update
issues.body: ### Summary The current footer on the Axios website (https://axios-http.com) looks visually inconsistent and somewhat cluttered due to large sponsor logos and uneven spacing. This reduces the overall design polish of the page. ### Observations - Sponsor logos vary in size and alignment. - The "Get Started" button and sponsor section overlap visually on smaller screens. - The footer section spacing and text alignment feel unbalanced compared to the clean, minimal Axios brand style. ### Suggested Improvements - Normalize sponsor logo sizes. - Add consistent vertical spacing between sponsor row and footer content. - Reduce or better align text-heavy sponsor labels. - Improve responsiveness for small screen widths. ### Screenshot ### Environment - Browser: Chrome 141.0 - OS: macOS 10.15.7 - Repo: axios/axios-docs --- I’d love to help fix this footer layout issue if it’s open for community contributions.
